response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior full-stack engineer with genuine strengths in testing culture, mentoring, and polyglot development, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, far short of the required 8 years. His large-scale distributed systems exposure (~2 years of low-latency DLang services at Dunnhumby) falls below the 5-year bar, and he has never held a formal technical leadership role owning platform strategy or leading cloud/DevOps engineers. Most of his career centers on web and product engineering in TypeScript/Node.js rather than cloud compute infrastructure.
strengths: Hands-on low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second)
Strong software craftsmanship and testing background (TDD, CI/CD, open-source test tooling, technical workshops and courses)
Broad polyglot experience across web, mobile, embedded, and systems programming with a track record of shipping products
concerns: Professional C++ experience is under one year, versus the 8 years required
No formal technical leadership role mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers or owning large ambiguous platform projects
Limited large-scale infrastructure and cloud architecture depth (~2 years versus the 5 years required)

key_factors.factor: Lack of technical leadership track record
